channel.title: Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: MSIX Per-User vs All Users: Install, Provision, and Uninstall Packages
item.description: MSIX supports making a package available to all users; the formal term is provisioning. Provisioning a package family makes it available to all users, whereas registration makes a package available to a single user. Per-User vs All Users Traditional installers often provide a "per-machine" installation mode to make softwar Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/insidemsix/msix-per-user-vs-all-users

item.title: Mailbox requirement set 1.16 now available for Outlook add-ins
item.description: Mailbox requirement set 1.16 is now generally available for Outlook add-ins. This release reflects our continued investment in closing the gap between COM/VSTO and web add-ins, with a focus on message and information security. Mail Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/mailbox-requirement-set-1-16-now-available-for-outlook-add-ins

item.title: A compatibility note on the abuse of Windows window class extra bytes
item.description: During my discussion of the evolution of system-windows window and class extra bytes, I noted that even though IDs are typically small integers, people liked to stash pointers there, so we Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260630-00/?p=112488

item.title: 🚀React Native Windows v0.84 is here!!
item.description: We're excited to release React Native Windows 0.84.0, aligned with React Native 0.84.1. React Native Windows v0.84 delivers input-handling improvements such as standard click events and imperative focus, theme-aware defaults and robust hit-testing semantics. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/react-native/react-native-windows-v0-84-is-here

item.title: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach End of Support on November 10, 2026
item.description: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach end of support on November 10, 2026. After that date, Microsoft will no longer provide servicing updates, security fixes, or technical support for these versions. We recommend upgrading to Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-8-9-end-of-support

item.title: SkiaSharp 4.0 is here: announcing the first stable release
item.description: SkiaSharp has been the backbone of cross-platform 2D graphics in .NET for over a decade. It powers pixel-perfect text, geometry, and image rendering across mobile, desktop, web, and server using the open-source Skia engine, and it sits under .NET MAUI, WebAssembly, WinUI 3, and frameworks like Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/skiasharp-4-0-stable

item.title: Packaging and Package Identity for .NET apps with WinApp CLI on Windows
item.description: Package identity has often been a pain point for developers looking to build apps that integrate with Windows APIs. Many modern Windows features, like push notifications or the AI APIs, are gated behind package identity. For Windows apps that are unpackaged by default (like .NET console or WPF applications), this meant wrestling with packag Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/packaging-dotnet-apps-winapp

item.title: WSL container is now available for public preview
item.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, we introduced WSL containers, bringing Linux container development directly into Windows through the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL). Containers have become a foundational part of modern development - from cloud-native applications and AI workloads to testing and deployment pipelines and WSL containers simplify th Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/wsl-container-is-now-available-for-public-preview

item.title: Automating your Visual Studio extension builds with GitHub Actions
item.description: If you’re building and maintaining Visual Studio extensions, you’ve probably ended up with some sort of build and publishing workflow - whether it’s manual, scripted, or stitched together over time. This post is for extension authors who want a simple, repeatable way to build, version, and publish their VSIX files using GitHub Actions Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/automating-your-visual-studio-extension-builds-with-github-actions

item.title: Agent Harness: Working with your data, safely
item.description: Part 2 of Build your own claw and agent harness with Microsoft Agent Framework. In Part 1 we stood up a har Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/agent-harness-working-with-your-data-safely

item.title: Remote Event Receivers are retiring: move to SharePoint webhooks before July 1, 2027
item.description: On July 1, 2027, Remote Event Receivers (RERs) will stop working in SharePoint Online. After that date, no remote event receiver will fire events, regardless of how it was registered. If your application still relies on remote event receivers to react to changes in SharePoint, now is the time to plan your move to Share Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/remote-event-receivers-are-retiring-move-to-sharepoint-webhooks-before-july-1-2027

item.title: Spring AI 2.0 is GA: Vector Search, Memory, and Agents on Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: The wait is over. Spring AI 2.0 is generally available, and Azure Cosmos DB is right there with it. With this release, Sp Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/spring-ai-2-0-is-ga-vector-search-memory-and-agents-on-azure-cosmos-db

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd) - May and June 2026
item.description: This is the combined May and June round-up for the Azure Developer CLI (azd). Nine releases shipped across the two months: 1.24.3, 1.25.0, Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/azure-developer-cli-azd-may-june-2026

item.title: The case of the DLL that was not present in memory despite not being formally unloaded, part 2
item.description: Last time, we looked at crashes caused by a DLL being removed from memory behind everybody's back, causing crashes when somebody tried to call into that no-longer-ther Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260626-00/?p=112472

item.title: Passing Context Between Agents in Multi-Agent A2A Systems
item.description: Introduction Multi-agent systems are becoming the go-to architecture for complex AI workloads. Instead of one monolithic agent doing everything, teams decompose capabilities into specialized agents — each with its own domain expertise, deployment pipeline, and release cadence. This approach mirrors the microservice patterns that ha Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/a2a-context-passing-multi-agent-systems

item.title: Your agent already has a plan
item.description: If an agent isn't doing the right thing, the obvious move is to make the docs clearer. Add a tip, spell out the correct command, describe the right approach more prominently. You do all of that, and the agent still ignores it. It does what it had already decided to do. The tip wasn't ignored because it was unclear, it was ignored beca Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/your-agent-already-has-a-plan

item.title: Iterating faster with TypeScript 7
item.description: How the VS Code and TypeScript teams collaborated to adopt TypeScript 7 and speed up VS Code development Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/iterating-faster-with-typescript-7

item.title: Save the Date: Pure Virtual C++ 2026
item.description: Pure Virtual C++ is back! For the 7th year running, we're hosting a free, one-day virtual conference for the whole C++ community. Whether you joined us back in 2020 when we first launched or you're discovering the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/save-the-date-pure-virtual-cpp-2026

item.title: Learn from Microsoft: Transform software development through an agentic platform
item.description: AI is already changing how teams build software, and the biggest shift goes beyond writing code faster. It’s reshaping the full software lifecycle, from planning and development to security, operations, and modernization. Inside Microsoft, we build and run software at massive scale, across every kind of codebase, and we put these agentic too Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/learn-from-microsoft-transform-software-development-through-an-agentic-platform

item.title: Raymond's hot take on Hainanese chicken
item.description: "If we give it no flavor, we can say that it's really subtle." Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260625-01/?p=112469

item.title: The case of the DLL that was not present in memory despite not being formally unloaded, part 1
item.description: The team responsible for shell32.dll received a bug saying that they were responsible for a large number of crashes in a particular third party program. Opening the crash dumps showed the clear signs of a stack overflow: # Child-SP RetAddr Call Site Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260625-00/?p=112467

item.title: Which Azure Cosmos DB Role Does My App Need?
item.description: In the previous post in the series, we covered the security decisions you make on day one. In this part, we will talk about how to give your app access to Cosmos DB data, using roles and a managed identity instead of keys Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/which-azure-cosmos-db-role-does-my-app-need

item.title: Azure Functions MCP Extension: What's New at Build 2026
item.description: The Azure Functions MCP extension provides simple abstractions to help you build and host MCP servers without having to learn the protocol details yourself. You can use the triggers and bindings model to expose any function as an MCP tool, resource, or prompt. Since its initial preview, the extension has grown from supporting a single trigger Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/functions-mcp-updates-build-2026

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.126
item.description: Learn what is new in Visual Studio Code 1.126 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.126

item.title: When the model has never seen your code
item.description: This is the fifth article in a series about Agent Experience (AX): the practice of making AI coding agents work correctly with your technology. The series covers what you can and can't control in the agent stack, how to measure whether your extensions are helping or hurting, and how to iterate toward better outcomes. Everyt Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/when-the-model-has-never-seen-your-code

item.title: Streamline C++ Code Intelligence Setup in Copilot CLI
item.description: C++ developers can now bring IDE-style semantic intelligence into Copilot CLI with less setup. The C++ language server plugin supports richer code navigation, diagnostics, symbol understanding, and code changes by using the same build context your compiler does. That build cont Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/streamline-c-code-intelligence-setup-in-copilot-cli

item.title: Add vs Stage and Register
item.description: As previously mentioned to 'install' a package, as people think of the term, Windows actually performs two separate operations: staging and registration. To oversimplify: Staging puts t Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/insidemsix/add-vs-stage-and-register

item.title: Going beyond text in Microsoft 365 Copilot - Introducing SharePoint Copilot Apps
item.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot is brilliant at working with language. But work isn't only words, sometimes you don't want to describe what you need in a back-and-forth chat - you want to see it, touch it, and act on it right w Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/going-beyond-text-in-microsoft-365-copilot-introducing-sharepoint-copilot-apps
